name: Embalse de las Torcas — circular desde Villanueva de Huerva
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Villanueva De Huerva offer diverse terrain shaped by the Huerva River, nestled between mountains, plains, and valleys. This region, located near Zaragoza, features a unique landscape with native pine forests, riverine environments, and distinctive "cabezos" (hills). The varied topography provides a range of routes suitable for mountain biking, from river-carved ravines to elevated viewpoints. Best mountain bike trails around Villanueva De Huerva The most popular mountain bike trail is Embalse de las Torcas — circular desde Villanueva de Huerva, a 43.6 miles (70.1 km) difficult trail that takes 6 hours 35 minutes to complete. This challenging route explores the wider regional landscape, including the Embalse de las Torcas. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Mountainbike loop from Valmadrid, a moderate 17.6 miles (28.3 km) path. This route offers a balanced ride through varied terrain, often passing through open landscapes and rural areas. Local mountain bikers also love the Antena de Valmadrid loop from Valmadrid, a 22.6 miles (36.4 km) trail leading through elevated areas with potential viewpoints, often completed in about 3 hours 5 minutes. Mountain biking around Villanueva De Huerva is defined by its river valleys, pine forests, and rolling hills. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels, catering to experienced riders. The routes in Villanueva De Huerva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.5 stars from 4 reviews. More than 20 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villanueva De Huerva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Villanueva de Huerva?
A: The terrain around Villanueva de Huerva is highly diverse, shaped by the Huerva River and its surrounding landscape. You'll encounter a mix of mountains, plains, and valleys, with trails traversing native pine forests, riverine environments, and distinctive 'cabezos' (hills). The routes often feature river-carved ravines and elevated areas offering panoramic views.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Villanueva de Huerva?
A: The mountain bike routes in Villanueva de Huerva are generally geared towards moderate to difficult ability levels, catering to experienced riders. While there are 5 moderate routes available, there are no trails specifically classified as easy. Riders should be comfortable with varied terrain and some elevation changes.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while mountain biking in the area?
A: The region is rich in natural and historical features. You can explore the unique landscape shaped by the Huerva River, including gorges and meanders. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muel Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5197207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torcas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The 'cabezos' (hills) of San Pablo and San Vicente offer panoramic views, and the El Paso site features fossilized dinosaur footprints. You might also encounter historical vestiges like Civil War trenches.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986751270/embalse-de-las-torcas-circular-desde-villanueva-de-huerva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de las Torcas — circular desde Villanueva de Huerva&lt;/a&gt;, which is 70.1 km long. Other moderate circular routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31883671" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountainbike loop from Valmadrid&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38178462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Antena de Valmadrid loop from Valmadrid&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Villanueva de Huerva?
A: The mountain bike trails in Villanueva de Huerva are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.5 stars from 4 reviews. Over 20 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscape, including river valleys, pine forests, and rolling hills.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Villanueva de Huerva?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations are not provided, the diverse landscape of Villanueva de Huerva, with its riverine environments and pine forests, suggests that spring and autumn would likely offer the most pleasant riding conditions, avoiding the extremes of summer heat or potential winter cold/wetness. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Can I find routes that offer good viewpoints?
A: Yes, the region's topography, with its 'cabezos' (hills) and elevated areas, provides opportunities for scenic viewpoints. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38178462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Antena de Valmadrid loop from Valmadrid&lt;/a&gt; leads through elevated areas with potential viewpoints. The Hermitage of San Vicente also offers panoramic views of the surrounding pine forest.

Q: Are there any historical sites or man-made monuments accessible from the trails?
A: Yes, the area has several historical and man-made attractions. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Dam of Muel&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5113006" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen de Dorleta Memorial&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8040166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Longares Mudejar Tower&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features archaeological sites on the 'cabezos' of San Pablo and San Vicente, and a medieval bridge along the Huerva River.

Q: How many mountain bike routes are available in Villanueva de Huerva?
A: There are 7 mountain bike routes available around Villanueva de Huerva on komoot. These routes range from moderate to difficult in terms of challenge, offering options for experienced riders to explore the varied landscape.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by a reservoir or lake?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986751270/embalse-de-las-torcas-circular-desde-villanueva-de-huerva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de las Torcas — circular desde Villanueva de Huerva&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that explores the wider regional landscape, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5197207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torcas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike route in the area?
A: The longest mountain bike route available is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986751270/embalse-de-las-torcas-circular-desde-villanueva-de-huerva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de las Torcas — circular desde Villanueva de Huerva&lt;/a&gt;, which covers a distance of 70.1 km and is classified as difficult.
tips: Near the town of Tosos and Aguilón we find ourselves in the dam with wonderful views of the Torcas Reservoir that is part of the Huerva River.
In the municipality of Tosos and not far from the Las Torcas Reservoir we find these historic ruins and the hermitage of El Santo, well declared Cultural Heritage of Aragon on November 26, 2002.
